text: Alice to the engineering leadership team: the Postgres decision for Atlas is final and ratified. I want everyone to understand why we chose Postgres over MongoDB. ACID compliance is the foundation of Atlas — we cannot have partial writes in the transaction ledger. This is a 5-year decision.

Bob confirming the Phoenix Kafka migration is complete. We are now officially off RabbitMQ. Kafka 3 brokers live in production on GCP Kubernetes. 600k messages per second throughput confirmed in production load test. Alice's Atlas events are flowing cleanly through the kafka atlas-events topic.

Frank: all Meridian infrastructure is now on GCP Kubernetes. The last AWS EC2 instances were decommissioned this morning. Terraform is managing everything. Docker containers for Atlas, Phoenix, Orion, Horizon, Nexus are all running in GKE Autopilot. The GCP enterprise agreement is signed and the 30% discount is active.

Carol to leadership: TypeScript strict mode is now enforced across all Horizon frontend code. The CI pipeline on GitHub now blocks merges on TypeScript errors. This was decided in January and we've now completed the migration. 47 React components are fully typed and documented in Storybook.

Dave update: Orion PyTorch model is now in production on GCP. Inference latency: 58ms p99. Prediction accuracy: 96% on the Atlas transaction dataset. The Airflow training pipeline is running nightly. Sam has started the LangChain spike for the Q3 natural language query feature. TBD on OpenAI vs self-hosted.

Eve to leadership team: Q1 wrap up. We delivered: Atlas on Postgres in production (Alice), Phoenix on Kafka (Bob), Horizon beta with TypeScript React (Carol), Orion alpha on PyTorch GCP (Dave), all infrastructure on GCP Kubernetes with Terraform (Frank). Five major decisions executed flawlessly. Series A is strong.

Alice: the v2 pricing model is still undecided. Eve and Nathan are working through the analysis. The open question is whether to meter Orion ML inference calls separately from Atlas transactions. Frank has the GCP unit cost data. Nathan has the competitive benchmark. Decision needed by end of Q2. This is blocking the enterprise sales motion.

Bob: the Prism Kafka Streams implementation is progressing. The real-time aggregates are working in staging — 1-minute rolling windows on Atlas transaction volume, consuming the atlas-events Kafka topic. The Postgres persistence layer is designed by Alice. Carol has the Horizon TypeScript charts ready to integrate. Q2 GA looks achievable.

Frank: EU expansion infrastructure planning. The GDPR requirement means Atlas Postgres data for EU customers must stay in GCP europe-west1. Rachel confirmed the compliance requirement. The Terraform configuration is ready. The question is timeline — this is TBD pending the GDPR audit Rachel is coordinating. Hiro will lead the deployment.

Nathan: competitive update — three of our five largest deals this quarter cited Postgres ACID compliance as the reason they chose Meridian over competitors. The competitors are using MongoDB and have had data consistency incidents. Alice's decision to use Postgres for Atlas is our biggest sales asset. We should highlight this in all enterprise messaging.

Alice: the Atlas bulk import feature is on track. Bob's Kafka consumer pipeline for bulk processing is designed. Customers upload CSV, Phoenix processes each row through the Atlas Postgres ledger. 100k transactions per batch in under 60 seconds is the target. This addresses the 7/20 customer interviews where Blake heard this request.

Morgan: Prism is going to GA in Q2. Kafka Streams real-time analytics, Postgres persistence, Carol's Horizon TypeScript charts. The architecture that Alice and Bob settled on — Kafka for real-time, Postgres for historical — is the right design. Customer demos of Prism are getting strong reactions. Eve, Nathan: please schedule enterprise demos of Prism alongside Atlas.

Rachel: security audit scope confirmed. Focus areas: Atlas Postgres encryption at rest (Frank to lead remediation), JWT token handling across all services (Frank), OAuth flow in Horizon (Carol), network policies on Titan Kubernetes (Frank). The audit starts next month. Alice: please make sure the Atlas Postgres config is audit-ready.

Eve: v2 roadmap confirmed by leadership and board. Q2: Atlas 50k TPS, Horizon v2 launch, Prism GA, Orion GA. Q3: Nexus gateway, Vega analytics, Zenith customer portal. Q4: International (GCP europe-west1), LangChain NLQ for Orion, Horizon i18n. Open decisions still needed: v2 pricing model (Eve/Nathan), LangChain backend (Dave/leadership), Vega: Postgres vs BigQuery.
